item.title: +1，Good article
item.content:encoded: Hello，Billith ，I'm a translator of scp-cn, and I'm translating your scp work now. Your writing is wonderful!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Kalinin’s point about the reveal of the chair god in the Interview Log. With a such ridiculously fun and interesting article that uses so much absurd comedy in it (i.e. the mahogany chair made of smaller chairs), I wish the reveal had a bit more of that absurdity in it. Still, this one is one of my new favorites, from the wonderful insanity it contains, along with it being written well. +1

Ayy lmao

Take some sweet sweet edits, dedicated to whoever got me the Divine Rating of 💯+♋.

Included are tonal improvements, new jokes and more realistic dialogue that sacrifices 0% of the charm.

Thanks fam

item.title: Re: Problem with Exploration Log 3311-A
item.content:encoded: Absolutely agreed, this really drew me out of it. Just too unbelievable that D-personnel that don't apparently have anything special about them are capable of this. My experience is the average person isn't happy with walking 20km in a day, let alone 100kms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Walking 200 kilometers over a day? Darn, please tell me she was at least equipped with a bicycle

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just aggravated because it messes up my understanding of the scene and makes me think that there is more than one entity there when there isn't. And with the nature of SCPs, anything could happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always give my Ds defined genders. My first non-binary D was literally in my first article, 2853. 2786 is genderfluid. Would you prefer I just refer to them by the gender of their current form? That kind of seems a little complicated

I don't know about you but I can't think of a better pronoun to use that isn't one of the two established ones available.

I honestly think that getting upset about it is rather pedantic. It's not like I'm the only one who does this. And English doesn't have an equivalent word afaik. I'm not qualified to argue about this though so moving on.

2950 was purposefully left out of this skip because it isn't actually a chair, but I could see them getting paired up in the next OCT lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As for the article, I am getting very tired of the use of plural pronouns to refer to singular entities. This has to stop.

That's probably not going to happen. There's a lexical need for a nonspecific gender neutral pronoun in English, and "they" only "doesn't work" because of prescriptivism based on applying Latin rules to English. Usage almost always evolves based on lexical need, and prescriptivists eventually lose these things (if they didn't we'd still all be speaking proto-Indo-European).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would love to see this be connected to SCP-2950 somehow. A chair that's just a chair but really isn't a chair. We define it to be a chair. And now the chair god has to deal with something that isn't his creation.

As for the article, I am getting very tired of the use of plural pronouns to refer to singular entities. This has to stop.
thanks for the support, this article is now my 3rd 100+ :))

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for this! I appreciate the kind words

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good sense of humor, takes itself just seriously enough, and the interview and logs are a good read. Overall, an A+ effort. Great job!

item.title: Problem with Exploration Log 3311-A
item.content:encoded:
I'm enjoying this. One thing I recommend changing, are the details of distance traveled. Currently D-9082 walks for ~100 km taking what must be about 24+ hours, before setting up camp for the first time. i.e.

Subject takes a few breaths and continues forward. The next fifty kilometers pass without incident

and

Subject continues for another forty kilometers over the next twelve hours without incident

They either need more food and time, or a shorter distance and number of repeaters. 240km at ave walking speed (3mph or 5kmh) would take 48 hours, which is 3 days at 16hours each of solid walking, and that's just outbound!
You could use this calculator to find appropriate numbers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article sits well with me.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why you don't let amateurs interrogate people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It's an infinite storage locker"

Meh

"It has every single chair"

Oh shit +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this entry almost as much as potato-verse! The exploration logs could use a bit of polishing, but some of that dialogue is immediately iconic, just brilliant stuff.

"The air is ripe with it." "With chair?" "…yes."

That's going to make me laugh for weeks. Easy +1.

item.title: I am a chair.
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly unsettling for how innocuous it seems. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coming from you, i have to say im flattered and feel like an imposter among gods

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a lot of fun to read, cryptic and strange without feeling illogical. I also appreciate that you could have gone full Bees but chose not to. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just feel like the point is moot as they were under the influence of a cognitohazard which was warping their thought processes to include a greater definition for what it means to be a chair. There are certain easter eggs that help give a bigger picture. i suppose they might be too subtle tho

Also Ive tried to give every one of the D-Class i write about a personality. Not necessarily based on me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno about you, but i joke when I'm nervous.

The plight of the author is trying to write characters that don't come off as just a warped facet of yourself. Because, see, just one person who's like that makes a lot of sense. Having every character use the same coping mechanisms and so on and so forth begins to feel like it's just what everyone is like (when in fact it's that everyone you write is like you). While I didn't have the same reaction as Kalinin did (take your upvote), I think their criticism makes sense. Not everyone gets jokey. Some get textbook style scared, or some go deathly silent. I dunno. I get what Kali's saying — I will say that I liked the second D-Class a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel similarly :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Saw this in the old Reddit fuel thread, and loved it.

Little grammatical thing:
Just a bit of a run-on that stuck out to me.

The camera feed jostles and is lost due to unknown reasons and contact with D-7820 is not reestablished.

should probably be…

The camera feed jostles and is lost due to unknown reasons. Contact with D-7820 is not reestablished.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool man. This really is a very, very strange but fun to read article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
almost too weird

incoherent

these are the compliments I live for

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Corrected!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a notice, the exploration tag is only for pages with explorations, not the SCP having an exploration in of itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's almost too weird for me. The interview at the end is almost incoherent. But there's enough personality here for my enjoyment.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: to be honest, that is exactly how I felt writing this. It was a little self indulgent, a little absurd, but I had a lot of fun with it. I was channeling the same spirit of weirdness that led me to write SCP-????-J. I suppose it could be rewritten, and maybe that is worth doing, but I'm going to keep this up and see how it does overall.

I had fun reading this. There were a lot of parts I've seen before, and a lot of parts where the dialogue felt unbelievable, but all of them were immediately followed by some enjoyably weird thing about chairs that got me back on board. It had ups and downs in every section, but I ended up really liking it overall.

Some SCPs are like fine exotic cuisine, while this is more like a really good cheeseburger. Which is sometimes exactly what you want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I really wanted to keep this one fun cause sometimes the sad ones bum me out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, I kinda feel like it's been a while since I saw "exploration of infinite realm of stuff", and I really enjoyed this. It's like the Bag of Potatoes, with chairs, and a little bit of Inside at the end there.

A mahogany chair made of other, smaller mahogany chairs

I mean, I pretty much had to upvote here, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the info! I'll take a close look at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I agree with Kalinin in that the concept of "portal to the infinite realm of [x]" is starting to become a cliche in the wake of 3008's success. Not that it's isn't a fun idea; just that now because of that, the bar is going to be set much higher in regards to what gets accepted and what gets labeled a lack-luster example of the trope.

I also agree that, as far as logs go, this one's were a little on the boring side. The dialogue wasn't that bad, but there also wasn't anything particularly interesting about these D's that I haven't seen many times before, and I found myself accurately predicting what was going to happen pretty easily. The only thing I was surprised about was how long it took to find the chair-ified D-Class. Called that one as soon as he put the coat over himself after it was established that "thing on top of other thing = chair".

All that said, I do like the novelty of the concept, and you did get a chuckle out of me in certain spots (the mental image of a herd of chairs galloping, mahogany chair made of smaller mahogany chairs). Novote for now, feel free to ping me if you polish it up any and I'll reconsider giving and upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno about you, but i joke when I'm nervous. I try to give all my Ds different personalities. Their cognitition is subtlely affected by the concept of chair, which comes off as silly and absurd.

I do agree that this one was more "formulaic" than some of my others, but honestly it's supposed to be silly and messed up and not a difficult read.

To each their own!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bypassing the fact that this is the latest entry in the ever-growing "infinite dimension that contains stuff" genre (which isn't really affecting my vote but not really exciting me all that much either), as a story this was somewhat predictable in terms of what happens to the D-Class. That on its own also doesn't really affect my vote either, but as a narrative it's not offering a whole lot to add to the initial concept.

Speaking of that, the initial concept isn't a bad one. There's a certain mystery as to why there are all these chairs in separate little units, which get more and more demonstrative of the whole "concept of chair" thing you're going for here. Where my downvote got sealed was the way in which the mystery is revealed, which was both way too literal for my tastes in terms of what's doing this and sort of a direct, mundane explanation for something so weird. I also didn't care much for the fact that you've got two D-Class expositing for us who both happen to enjoy pontificating despite the dangerous and unearthly situation that they're in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I'll edit those now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*nervously checks the chair I'm currently sitting on*

+1; I never before realised that I could be made to fear chairs.

+ Nitpicking goes here
- Sorry, I'm a pedant

I fixed a couple of typos, & found a couple of other points where the word usage looks off but I wasn't sure enough of your original intent to change it - so, requesting clarification on those points:

SCP-3311's walls are lined with cubical compartments of varying size

Did you mean "cubicle" as in a partitioned-off area of a room (e.g. an office cubicle), or did you mean "shaped like a cube" (in which case I'd suggest using "cubic" to avoid this confusion)?

Subject removes camera and places it on her side

This gives me images of D-9082 lying on one side with the camera atop her, which seems… odd. Is this what you had in mind, or were you going for "by her side" or possibly "on its side"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: haha, yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chairs for the chair god, thrones for the…throne, throne?

+1

item.title: haha, so...
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is a thing.

The place where the concept of chair is infinitely dense

Based on an old fuel post with my fam Heavenfall, who gave permission to rework this idea. I'd also be lying if i said my gf didn't contribute her wacky ideas too.

The name "All the Little Chairs" is a reference to the adventure time episode "All the Little People", where Finn plays god with figurine-sized versions of the characters.

Photo is flickr, cc
https://www.flickr.com/photos/34517490@N00/37668352432/in/photolist-Y4cb2s-VHjCiP-XYrSca-21jUvcW-YEeNaq-cc8MTd-cc8NEW-m2Fc7P-bULAmx-cc8QFy-bULAdz-hgz94V-bULzTD-aczLop-dBhR2Y-bzBgHk-dBcrGx-dBhWZ9-5C7vTX-bULx9X-Hnr18k-YAWVQ5-hYnn66-BNMTqs-YWe2fn-WNyAEv-fzTZt-8V8Fyv-ZoC9Qh-MEdDE-8LvjmB-cc8Ny7-bULwy6-hYnRdY-cc8Q5W-7CXDb7-cc8Pom-bULzbt-cc8QwQ-8LvkdV-cc8NQh-mqJXg6-mqJyYF-mqJrRK-mqKGW3-mqJgjV-A9RzA5-Pbetmh-v3h5AM-dBcvdt

Shoutout to C-Dives for translating All the Little Chairs into Japanese!
